title: TRIED Verification API Contract
description: HTTP request and response shapes for the four endpoints exposed by the Lenovo verification server. Enforced as Pydantic models in packages/shared/src/shared/verification/api.py. Both machines (orchestrator on MacBook, server on Lenovo) import from that module — this file is the single source of truth for the wire format.

preflight_request.description: One-time check per job sent before the agent loop starts. The server runs PyTorch eager and torch.compile+Inductor on the same inputs and verifies they agree within the requested tolerance. If they do not, the example is written to skipped.jsonl and the agent loop never starts.

pytorch_code.description: Standalone PyTorch function to evaluate. Must be executable with import torch only.

preflight_response.description: Result of the eager-vs-Inductor sanity check. passed=true means references agree and the agent loop may start. passed=false means the example is a skip. error_message is set when the harness itself failed (import error, shape error, OOM, etc.) rather than a tolerance disagreement.

passed.description: True iff eager and Inductor outputs agree within tolerance_policy. False on tolerance failure or harness error.

vs_eager_inductor.description: Agreement stats between eager and Inductor. Null when a harness error prevented comparison.

error_message.description: Non-null only when the harness itself failed (not a tolerance failure). Null on clean pass or clean tolerance failure.

eager_first_call_ms.description: Wall time of the first eager invocation of pytorch_code, CUDA-event timed. Populated when the eager call ran without throwing; null iff the harness errored before that call. Eval-gen pipeline records this in EvalCorpusRecord.acceptance; dataset pipeline ignores it.

inductor_first_call_ms.description: Wall time of the first torch.compile(backend='inductor') invocation, CUDA-event timed. Dominated by Inductor cold-compile (typically 60–120 s). Populated when the Inductor call ran; null on earlier harness failure.

compile_request.description: Attempt to compile a generated Triton kernel. Called before /run to get a fast failure signal — compile errors are the most common failure mode for LLM-generated code.

triton_code.description: Raw Triton kernel source produced by the generator. Sent verbatim from the orchestrator.

compile_response.description: Result of attempting to compile the Triton kernel. On success the kernel is in the Triton JIT cache keyed by source hash; subsequent /run or /benchmark calls with the same triton_code will hit the cache.

error_message.description: Captured compiler error text. Non-null iff status == 'failed'.

run_request.description: Run the compiled kernel and compare its output against PyTorch eager and torch.compile+Inductor. The server reconstructs input tensors from shapes, dtypes, and rng_seed — tensors are never sent over the wire.

triton_code.description: Same kernel source sent to /compile. Triton JIT cache will be a hit if /compile was called first with identical code.

pytorch_code.description: PyTorch reference function used to generate the eager and Inductor outputs for comparison.

run_response.description: Correctness comparison between the Triton candidate and both reference implementations. All fields except error_message are null when a runtime error (not a compile error) prevented execution.

correctness_status.description: Whether the Triton output agreed with PyTorch eager within tolerance. Null on runtime error.

tolerance_policy_used.description: The policy key actually used for this comparison. Echoed from the request. Null on runtime error.

vs_eager.description: Primary comparison: Triton vs PyTorch eager. Null on runtime error.

vs_inductor.description: Secondary comparison: Triton vs torch.compile+Inductor. Null on runtime error.

error_message.description: Non-null only when a runtime error prevented execution. Null on both clean pass and clean numeric failure.

benchmark_request.description: Time a correctness-passing kernel against eager and Inductor baselines. Caller must have confirmed correctness_status == 'passed' from /run before calling this endpoint. Returns a job_accepted immediately (HTTP 202); poll GET /jobs/{job_id} for the result. Protocol: 10 warmup runs (not timed), 100 timed runs, median reported, CUDA events used, synchronized start and end.

triton_code.description: Same kernel source used in /compile and /run. Triton JIT cache will be a hit.

pytorch_code.description: PyTorch reference function. Used to time eager and Inductor baselines.

benchmark_response.description: Timing results for the Triton kernel and both baselines. Returned as the result field inside job_status once the job reaches done. Speedups are the primary metric; absolute ms are recorded for reproducibility. The eval pipeline additionally consumes the *_samples_ms arrays for non-parametric paired tests.

triton_ms.description: Median wall time of the Triton kernel across 100 runs, in milliseconds.

eager_ms.description: Median wall time of PyTorch eager across 100 runs, in milliseconds.

inductor_ms.description: Median wall time of torch.compile+Inductor across 100 runs, in milliseconds.

speedup_vs_eager.description: eager_ms / triton_ms. Values >1.0 mean Triton is faster than eager.

speedup_vs_inductor.description: inductor_ms / triton_ms. Values >1.0 mean Triton beats Inductor.

triton_std_ms.description: Standard deviation of the 100 Triton timing runs, in milliseconds.

eager_std_ms.description: Standard deviation of the 100 eager timing runs, in milliseconds.

inductor_std_ms.description: Standard deviation of the 100 Inductor timing runs, in milliseconds.

triton_samples_ms.description: All 100 timed-iteration measurements for the Triton kernel. Required by EvalRecord.attempts[].benchmark for non-parametric paired tests and IQR-based descriptive stats.

eager_samples_ms.description: All 100 timed-iteration measurements for PyTorch eager.

inductor_samples_ms.description: All 100 timed-iteration measurements for torch.compile + Inductor.

job_accepted.description: Immediate response (HTTP 202) from POST /benchmark. The orchestrator uses job_id to poll GET /jobs/{job_id} until the job reaches done or error.

job_id.description: UUID identifying this benchmark job. Stable for the lifetime of the server process.

job_status.description: Response from GET /jobs/{job_id}. Orchestrator polls this until status is done or error.

result.description: The benchmark result. Non-null only when status == done.

error_message.description: Non-null only when status == error. Null otherwise.

job_status_value.description: Lifecycle of an async benchmark job. pending: queued, not started. running: actively executing. done: result available. error: failed, check error_message.

correctness_stats.description: Five-number agreement summary between two output tensors. Same shape as correctness_stats in dataset_record.json.

max_rel_diff.description: max(|a - b| / (|b| + eps)) over all elements.

n_elements_exceeding_tol.description: Count of elements that fail the tolerance policy.

pct_elements_exceeding_tol.description: n_elements_exceeding_tol as a percentage of total elements.

input_shapes.description: Shape of each input tensor. Length must equal length of input_dtypes.

input_dtypes.description: Dtype of each input tensor. Length must equal length of input_shapes.

rng_seed.description: RNG seed used to reconstruct input tensors on the server. Must match the value in the corpus record.

dtype.description: Input tensor dtype. Closed vocabulary — matches Dtype enum in enums.py.

tolerance_policy.description: Key into packages/shared/src/shared/verification/tolerance.py. Closed vocabulary — that module is the source of truth for atol/rtol values and comparison semantics.

compile_status.description: Closed vocabulary — matches CompileStatus enum in enums.py.

correctness_status.description: Closed vocabulary — matches CorrectnessStatus enum in enums.py.
